title: Dissociative Disorders
subtitle: The nurse supports a patient who disconnects from memory, identity, or reality — often after trauma. Ensure safety, build trust, and use grounding.

patient.name: Dissociative Disorders
patient.admitDate: The nurse supports a patient with a dissociative disorder.

nursesNote: Dissociative disorders involve a disconnection from memory, identity, or reality, usually as a way of coping with past trauma or abuse. Dissociative amnesia is an inability to recall important information, often around a traumatic event; dissociative identity disorder involves two or more distinct identities with gaps in memory; and depersonalization/derealization is feeling detached from oneself or that surroundings are unreal. The nurse first ensures safety, since self-harm and suicide risk can be high, then builds trust with a calm, consistent, predictable approach. During an episode the nurse helps the patient ground to the here-and-now and does not force recall of traumatic memories, letting the patient lead. Care supports trauma-focused therapy over time.
providerOrders: Assess safety and risk of self-harm first
Build trust with a calm, consistent approach
Use grounding to orient the patient during episodes
Do not force recall of traumatic memories

steps.title: Recognize Cues
steps.sub: What is true about dissociative disorders?

opts.text: They involve disconnection from memory or identity
They are often linked to past trauma
Dissociative amnesia is loss of key memories
Safety is rarely a concern
DID involves two or more identities
Grounding helps during an episode
Assess for self-harm and suicide risk
A calm, consistent approach builds trust
Forcing memories speeds recovery
Grounding orients the patient to the present
Depersonalization is feeling detached from self
Trauma-focused therapy is the long-term treatment

steps.title: Prioritize Hypotheses
steps.sub: Order caring for a patient who is dissociating

opts.text: Ensure safety and assess harm risk
Stay calm and speak gently
Ground the patient to the here-and-now
Reassure and build trust
Support therapy and document
Cannot recall events around a trauma
Feeling detached, like watching yourself
Two distinct identities with memory gaps
Surroundings feel dreamlike or unreal
Gaps in memory for a whole time period
Feeling emotionally numb and outside the body

steps.title: Take Action
steps.sub: A patient appears detached and unaware of surroundings

opts.text: Ensure the patient's safety
Speak calmly and use the patient's name
Ground: orient to who and where they are
Stay present and reassure
Document and follow up
Assessing for self-harm risk
Forcing the patient to recall the trauma
Using grounding during an episode
Being inconsistent and unpredictable
Building trust with a calm approach
Dismissing the experience as fake
Supporting trauma-focused therapy
Leaving a high-risk patient unmonitored
